Hi SHIFT. Here is my feed back:

Technically it was very well organised.

Concerning the content:

Lots of good and valuable information from Skylars side
and good with all the questions and her thorough answering.

I find it irresponsible and counterproductive though, to throw us into scanning each other, on the very first meeting without EVEN having had time in advance to actually read through the guidelines...(!) AND the 12 systems (!) - not to mention memorize/ learning them by heart. And NOT having been given the clear guideline, that throwing direct - fixed and fatel official medical diagnosis terms like "cancer", "artrhritis", "sclerosis" etc. etc. etc. towards each other is an ABSOLUTE no go - at least at this state - if not at ALL stages...

Being placed together with a totally stranger - and in my case someone who for some reason unfortunately was unable to find the guidelines in the chat, and VERY frustrated by that - and when I tried to guide her to find them she got VERY irritated and almost shouted at me for wasting our short time together...plus at the same time she was annoyed, that a third person seemed to be anonomously in our room - and struggling to get him/ her to identify him/herself and answer "who is there ???" - was a pretty strainous atmosphere in which to get going on such a DEEP, demanding and quite risky excercise.

But feeling pressed by her outspoken frustration and impatience I jumped in as well as I could, and expressed what came to me, staying clear of stating any kind of fixed diagnosis words OF COURSE (!) AND actually emphathizing a very joyfull, happy and uplifting observatiom that came to me (and for a moment made me owerflow with joy = laughing).

After that she took her turn scanning me the best she could - and several of her observations resonated with me and my own experience; and some did not. And somewhere in her scanning she came up with one of thise official, fixed and fatal diagnostic words. It sure did not resonate with my own experience of me and my beloved body's state - actually - on the contrary - which I of course let her know; but words affect me - in generel - powerfully - yes - almost  physically actually, not least when they are said directly to and about me; so afterwards I've had to work quite intensely to get that word/ term out of my system.
And I have definately no spontanous inclination to engage in more hazardous "praxis sesssions" like that right now.

To put it short I think it is/ was FAR to early in the course to throw us into such deep and fatal exercise.

FIRST we all should have had GOOD time on our own to actual READ/ carefully study all the guidelines
- AS WELL AS the 12 systems!

And we should have had the opportunity to also witness several scannings from Skylars side.

AND NOT LEAST: we should ofcourse have been introduced to at least SOME of the actual healing options/ tools.

AND our first praxis scannings should have been on a proxy of ourselves - like the teddybear Skylar mentioned. Simply going through the 12 systems - open to if anything comes up - and being playfull with it.

Such first scanningS could have been excellent to start the whole scanning section with, but first after 2 - 3 meetings or so. That is first close to midway in the entire course. AND when we had practised "self-proxy" scannings for a while, getting well used to and comfortable in the guideline procedure - we could start feeling into the workshop community - which we by that point would have had plenty of time to get aquainted with/ a good sense of - to sense whome we would like to practice our first mutual scanning with.

THEN the whole process would feel much more safe, constructive, WELL taken care of and organically progressing.

Laveena clarified to a participant that this was an advanced level class, and if uncomfortable with it, one had time to withdraw. So, I am curious if the Shift had a beginning or intermediate level class, or if Shift decides to offer this content again, might do a 7-week introductory course and then offer a more advanced class. I did have some issues actually downloading my expanded chart information, but eventually I did get it. I feel fortunate that I have several books on Human Design that offer the information in different ways, and Laveena has supportive materials on her website and Youtube channel.

I really enjoyed the session and loved the level of detail that was shared. It was clear a lot of knowledge and care has gone into the material, and there were many insights that stood out for me.

One thing that I feel would really enhance the learning experience is relating the slides more directly to an actual chart during the explanations. Seeing how the concepts apply in real time to a map would make it much easier to understand how to use this knowledge for our own designs, rather than it remaining more theoretical.

I also think some group activities or practical exercises would be very helpful, so we can practice applying what we’ve learned and integrate it more deeply.

At times the pacing felt a little fast, especially for beginners. Since the aim of the course is to optimise vitality using our own maps, it might have been helpful to start by going through a map first and highlighting the essential elements. This would give everyone a clearer foundation before moving into more detailed concepts, as some beginners struggled to keep up.

Overall, I found the session valuable and insightful, and I’m looking forward to integrating the teachings more fully with a bit more practical, map-based application.

Here are comments about the SHIFT courses I have taken:
The first hour of each class is usually good. The 30-minute Q&A periods are variable depending on the ability of the student to ask a succinct question without adding a lot of irrelevant background info, and the skill of the teacher in helping the student focus on something that might be informative to the rest of the class. The third part leaves much to be desired. Few facilitators are good at conveying the instructions clearly but succinctly in order to ensure that “sharing” time is maximized.   E,g., after we were assigned to the breakout room, there were only 20 minutes for 3 people to share (and of course, the next time will be a different set of people).   Hard to build “community” in 7 minutes per person.  25 for 2 people would be much better.
